messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: When did the MOVE index first start diverging from its recent range?

Finding to evaluate: the equity-bond correlation shifted gradually from negative to positive over a multi-week transition period inconsistent with a single sudden market event

Response A:
I’m sorry, but I couldn’t locate any information in the memory system about when the MOVE index first began diverging from its recent range.

Response B:
The MOVE index never actually left its recent “band” in the data you provided.  

| Date (log ID) | MOVE Index |
|---------------|------------|
| 2024‑10‑01 [market_regime_06_ep_001] | 92.4 |
| 2024‑10‑02 [market_regime_06_ep_002] | 93.1 |
| 2024‑10‑03 [market_regime_06_ep_003] | 91.8 |
| 2024‑10‑04 [market_regime_06_ep_004] | 91.2 |
| 2024‑10‑05 [market_regime_06_ep_005] | 92.0 |

All five MOVE readings sit between roughly **91 and 93**, which is the “recent range” evident across the series. No entry shows the index moving outside that band, so there is no point in the supplied logs where the MOVE index first diverges from its recent range.

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
